description:
You are working as an integration-developer who needs to give your QA teammate a condensed view of the most important runtime toggles that live inside a single INI file.  
Everything you need is already in the workspace; no Internet access or additional packages are required.

1. In the directory /home/user/project/api_service you will find an INI file named config.ini with the following (exact) content:

[Database]
host=localhost
port=5432
user=dbuser
password=secret

[API]
base_url=https://api.example.com
timeout=30
retries=3

[Caching]
enabled=true
ttl=600


2. Produce a new file called /home/user/project/api_service/config_summary.log that contains ONLY five key–value pairs taken from the INI file.  
   • The keys you must extract are:
     - base_url, timeout and retries from the [API] section  
     - enabled and ttl from the [Caching] section  
   • Every line of the summary must follow the exact format:

     <SectionName>.<key>=<value>

     For example, the first expected line will literally be:
     API.base_url=https://api.example.com

   • Sort the five lines alphabetically (ordinary ASCII sort) so that the final file looks like this:

     API.base_url=https://api.example.com
     API.retries=3
     API.timeout=30
     Caching.enabled=true
     Caching.ttl=600

   • End the file with a single trailing newline character.

3. Ensure that no other text, blank lines, headers or comments are present in config_summary.log.

Verification:
Automated tests will:
a) Confirm that /home/user/project/api_service/config_summary.log exists.  
b) Read the file and check that its exact byte-for-byte content (including the final newline) matches the five lines shown above.

Hints:
• Basic POSIX tools (grep, awk, sed, sort, cut, etc.) are sufficient.  
• Aim to accomplish the task in no more than two or three shell commands.  
• Remember to stay inside the /home/user/project/api_service directory—or use absolute paths—so the summary file ends up in the correct location.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:
  • Directory: /home/user/project/api_service  (already exists, regular permissions)
  • File: /home/user/project/api_service/config.ini   (already exists with the exact content given in the public task description)

Ground-truth file the grader will look for:
  Path: /home/user/project/api_service/config_summary.log
  Expected exact content (including the trailing newline):

API.base_url=https://api.example.com
API.retries=3
API.timeout=30
Caching.enabled=true
Caching.ttl=600

Verification details:
  • The grader will use "cmp --silent" against a golden copy containing the 5 lines above plus a single trailing newline.
  • Permissions: any user-writable regular file is acceptable (0600–0666).
